message.content:
{
  "homework": {
    "sessionInformation": {
      "sessionNumber": "3",
      "sessionTitle": "Number System - Session 3",
      "subject": "Mathematics",
      "grade": "Class IX",
      "difficultyLevel": "Balanced",
      "learningPace": "Balanced",
      "estimatedHomeworkDuration": "45 minutes"
    },
    "homework": [
      {
        "id": 1,
        "type": "Numerical Problem",
        "title": "Decimal Expansion of Rational Numbers",
        "learningOutcomeIds": ["LO_3.1"],
        "topicCoverage": ["Rational Numbers", "Decimal Expansions"],
        "difficulty": "Medium",
        "marks": 2,
        "estimatedTime": "8 minutes",
        "instructions": "Find the decimal expansion of the following rational numbers. State whether they have a terminating or non-terminating repeating decimal expansion.",
        "question": "a) 36/100\nb) 1/11\nc) 4/5",
        "options": [],
        "answerSpace": "Write the decimal value and classify it as 'Terminating' or 'Non-terminating Repeating'.",
        "visualRequirement": "",
        "expectedResponse": "a) 0.36 (Terminating), b) 0.0909... (Non-terminating repeating), c) 0.8 (Terminating)"
      },
      {
        "id": 2,
        "type": "Assertion Reason",
        "title": "Understanding Rational Numbers on the Number Line",
        "learningOutcomeIds": ["LO_3.2"],
        "topicCoverage": ["Rational Numbers", "Number Line Representation"],
        "difficulty": "Medium",
        "marks": 2,
        "estimatedTime": "10 minutes",
        "instructions": "Read the Assertion (A) and Reason (R) carefully. Choose the correct option from the choices given below.",
        "question": "Assertion (A): Every rational number can be represented as a point on the number line.\nReason (R): Rational numbers are of the form p/q where q is not zero.",
        "options": [
          "Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A",
          "Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A",
          "A is true but R is false",
          "A is false but R is true"
        ],
        "answerSpace": "Select the correct option.",
        "visualRequirement": "Draw a number line showing the position of 3/4 and -2/5 to support your reasoning if needed.",
        "expectedResponse": "Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A"
      },
      {
        "id": 3,
        "type": "Short Answer",
        "title": "Real-World Application of Rational Numbers",
        "learningOutcomeIds": ["LO_3.3"],
        "topicCoverage": ["Application of Rational Numbers"],
        "difficulty": "Medium",
        "marks": 2,
        "estimatedTime": "7 minutes",
        "instructions": "Solve the following real-life problem using rational numbers.",
        "question": "A tank is filled by two pipes. Pipe A fills 1/3 of the tank in one hour, and Pipe B fills 1/4 of the tank in one hour. If both pipes are opened together, what fraction of the tank will be filled in one hour?",
        "options": [],
        "answerSpace": "Show your calculation steps.",
        "visualRequirement": "Draw a simple diagram representing the tank and the two pipes filling it.",
        "expectedResponse": "1/3 + 1/4 = 4/12 + 3/12 = 7/12. The tank will be filled by 7/12 in one hour."
      },
      {
        "id": 4,
        "type": "Numerical Problem",
        "title": "Operations with Rational Numbers",
        "learningOutcomeIds": ["LO_3.4"],
        "topicCoverage": ["Addition and Subtraction of Rational Numbers"],
        "difficulty": "Medium",
        "marks": 2,
        "estimatedTime": "10 minutes",
        "instructions": "Perform the following operations and simplify your answer.",
        "question": "a) (-5/7) + (3/14)\nb) (2/3) - (-1/6)",
        "options": [],
        "answerSpace": "Show the common denominator method used.",
        "visualRequirement": "",
        "expectedResponse": "a) -10/14 + 3/14 = -7/14 = -1/2\nb) 4/6 + 1/6 = 5/6"
      },
      {
        "id": 5,
        "type": "Case Study",
        "title": "Analyzing Patterns in Decimal Expansions",
        "learningOutcomeIds": ["LO_3.5"],
        "topicCoverage": ["Patterns in Rational Numbers"],
        "difficulty": "High",
        "marks": 2,
        "estimatedTime": "10 minutes",
        "instructions": "Read the case study and answer the questions below.",
        "question": "Case: A student observes that when dividing 1 by prime numbers like 7, 13, etc., the decimal expansion repeats. However, when dividing by 2 or 5, it terminates.\nQuestion: Explain why the denominator's prime factors determine whether a rational number has a terminating or non-terminating repeating decimal expansion.",
        "options": [],
        "answerSpace": "Write a short explanation (3-4 lines).",
        "visualRequirement": "Create a small table comparing denominators 2, 5, 7, and 13 with their decimal types.",
        "expectedResponse": "If the prime factorization of the denominator contains only 2s and/or 5s, the decimal terminates. If it contains any other prime factor, the decimal repeats."
      }
    ],
    "summary": {
      "totalQuestions": 5,
      "totalMarks": 10,
      "estimatedCompletionTime": "45 minutes",
      "learningOutcomesCovered": [
        "LO_3.1: Identify terminating and non-terminating decimal expansions",
        "LO_3.2: Represent rational numbers on the number line",
        "LO_3.3: Apply rational numbers to real-world problems",
        "LO_3.4: Perform addition and subtraction of rational numbers",
        "LO_3.5: Analyze patterns in decimal expansions"
      ],
      "topicsCovered": [
        "Rational Numbers",
        "Decimal Expansions",
        "Number Line Representation",
        "Application of Rational Numbers",
        "Addition and Subtraction of Rational Numbers",
        "Patterns in Rational Numbers"
      ],
      "subtopicsCovered": [
        "Terminating vs Non-terminating Repeating",
        "Fractional Parts",
        "Common Denominators",
        "Prime Factorization in Denominators"
      ],
      "taskDistribution": {
        "Numerical Problem": 2,
        "Assertion Reason": 1,
        "Short Answer": 1,
        "Case Study": 1
      },
      "homeExperimentIncluded": false,
      "parentEngagementIncluded": false
    }
  }
}
